chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْحُبِّ فِي اللَّهِ وَ الْبُغْضِ فِي اللَّهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 60 – The love regarding Allah<sup>azwj</sup> and the hatred regarding Allah<sup>azwj</sup>

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (257/ 8)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (257/ 8)
text: مِنْ أَوْثَقِ عُرَى الْإِيمَانِ أَنْ تُحِبَّ فِي اللَّهِ وَ تُبْغِضَ فِي اللَّهِ وَ تُعْطِيَ فِي اللَّهِ وَ تَمْنَعَ فِي اللَّهِ .

en.hubeali: Ibn Mahboub, from Malik Bin Atiyya, from Saeed Al A’araj,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘From the strongest bonds of Emān is that you love regarding Allahazwj and hate regarding Allahazwj, and you give regarding Allahazwj and you deprive regarding Allahazwj’.
en.sarwar: 2. Ibn Mahbub has narrated from Malik ibn ‘Atiyah from Sa’id al-A’raj from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “The strongest ring of belief to hold onto is to love for the sake of Allah and hate for the sake of Allah, to give for the sake of Allah and deny for the sake of Allah.”
